channel.title: NYT > Top Stories
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Virginia Passes Gerrymandered Map to Help Democrats in Midterms: 4 Takeaways
item.description: Virginia’s approval of an aggressive gerrymander could give Democrats up to four additional House seats as they seek to win back Congress.
item.dc:creator: Reid J. Epstein
item.media:description: National party leaders were heavily invested in Virginia’s referendum, and tens of millions of dollars flowed in.

item.title: Here’s What the New Virginia House Map Looks Like
item.description: Democrats now hold six of the state’s 11 seats in the U.S. House, but the new map could allow the party to win 10 of them.
item.dc:creator: Elena Shao

item.title: Here’s Where the National Fight Over Gerrymandered Maps Stands
item.description: With Virginia’s vote on Tuesday, Democrats pulled close to even with Republicans, who may have further cards to play in the race to gain extra House seats.
item.dc:creator: Nick Corasaniti

item.title: What I Saw Crossing Into South Lebanon
item.description: Our visual journalist David Guttenfelder traveled with displaced people returning to their homes in southern Lebanon, as a cease-fire paused the fighting between Israel and Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: David Guttenfelder, Coleman Lowndes, Christina Thornell, Sutton Raphael, Joey Sendaydiego and Thomas Vollkommer

item.title: Trust Trump? Iran’s Doubts Shadow Peace Talks.
item.description: Iranian leaders fear being burned again by President Trump, who tore up a nuclear agreement reached during the Obama administration after lengthy negotiations.
item.dc:creator: Michael Crowley
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Vice President JD Vance during peace talks with Iran in Islamabad earlier this month.

item.title: U.S. Turns Up Pressure on Iraq to Distance Itself From Iran
item.description: Washington is demanding that the Baghdad government dismantle Iran-backed Iraqi militias that have been attacking Americans and U.S. sites there recently.
item.dc:creator: Raja Abdulrahim, Falih Hassan, Edward Wong and Erika Solomon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Members of Kataib Hezbollah, an Iran-backed Iraqi militia, during a mourning procession in Baghdad this month for one of their comrades who was killed.

item.title: Justice Dept. Charges Prominent Civil Rights Group With Financial Crimes
item.description: Republicans have accused the Southern Poverty Law Center, which is best known for investigating hate groups, of unfairly targeting conservative and Christian organizations.
item.dc:creator: Devlin Barrett
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Southern Poverty Law Center said that it was being investigated by the Justice Department over its past use of paid informants to infiltrate extremist groups.

item.title: U.S. Abruptly Rescinds Subpoenas in John Brennan Inquiry
item.description: The reversal came after the Justice Department replaced a career prosecutor with a loyalist to President Trump in the administration’s effort to charge the former C.I.A. chief with a crime.
item.dc:creator: Charlie Savage
item.category: Russian Interference in 2016 US Elections and Ties to Trump Associates
Federal Criminal Case Against Trump (Documents Case)
item.media:description: John O. Brennan testifying to Congress in 2017.

item.title: Trump Is Said to Be in Talks to Send Afghans Who Aided U.S. Forces to Congo
item.description: A U.S. aid worker said that the Afghans, who were evacuated to Qatar, would face a choice between moving to the Democratic Republic of Congo and living under the Taliban.
item.dc:creator: Megha Rajagopalan, Eileen Sullivan and Zolan Kanno-Youngs
item.media:credit: Sgt. Jimmie Baker/U.S. Army, via Getty Images
item.media:description: A photograph released by the U.S. Army showing Afghans being processed at Camp As Sayliyah, in Qatar, in August 2021.

item.title: Tucker Carlson Says He Is ‘Tormented’ by His Past Support for Trump
item.description: “I want to say I’m sorry for misleading people,” said the conservative commentator, who has broken sharply with the president over the war with Iran.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Tucker Carlson, the former Fox News host, has feuded with President Trump and his allies for weeks over the war with Iran.

item.title: RFK Jr. Refused to Commit to Backing New CDC Director on Vaccines
item.description: In a tense congressional hearing, the health secretary also said he bore no responsibility for the measles outbreak in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Christina Jewett and Sheryl Gay Stolberg
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
item.media:description: Robert F. Kennedy Jr. pushed back on Democrats’ assertions that he was responsible for the country’s worst measles outbreak in decades.

item.title: D.H.S. Will Run Out of Money for Paychecks in May, Secretary Says
item.description: The issue threatens to renew chaos at airports as lawmakers remain divided over a deal to end the two-month shutdown of the Department of Homeland Security.
item.dc:creator: Madeleine Ngo and Michael Gold
item.media:description: A Transportation Security Administration officer at Pittsburgh International Airport in March, when some U.S. travelers faced long security lines at airports.

item.title: Could ‘A River Runs Through It’ Have Been a Hit Today?
item.description: The autobiographical novella, first published 50 years ago, arguably created a new type of guy: the literary fly fisherman.
item.dc:creator: Monte Burke

item.title: Two Drugs Stir Hope for Treatment of Deadly Pancreatic Cancer
item.description: In separate small clinical trials, two treatments showed promise that they could help patients with one of the most dire diagnoses in oncology.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Robbins and Gina Kolata
item.media:description: An animated image showing the human body highlighting the pancreas.

item.title: Proposed Lifetime Smoking Ban to Become Law in Britain
item.description: The proposal, which was approved by Parliament on Tuesday, will ban the supply or sale of tobacco products to anyone born in 2009 or after, permanently.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.category: Charles III, King of the United Kingdom
item.media:description: The proposed lifetime smoking ban will apply in England, Scotland, Wales and Northern Ireland.

item.title: A Year After Terrorist Attack, a Kashmir Town Longs for Tourists
item.description: Pahalgam, with steep ravines, grassy hillsides and pine forests, is a base for Hindu pilgrimages that business operators hope will revive visitors.
item.dc:creator: Showkat Nanda
item.media:credit: Showkat Nanda for The New York Times
item.media:description: Security officers are a constant presence on tourist routes in Pahalgam after last year’s attack.

item.title: Federal Appeals Court Upholds Texas Ten Commandments Law
item.description: The U.S. Court of Appeals for the Fifth Circuit said the law does not violate the Constitution. The plaintiffs said they planned to ask the Supreme Court to reverse the decision.
item.dc:creator: Pooja Salhotra
item.media:credit: Desiree Rios for The New York Times
item.media:description: A Ten Commandments poster in an English classroom at Fossil Ridge High School in Fort Worth.

item.title: Dave Mason, Songwriter Who Co-Founded Traffic, Dies at 79
item.description: The English singer and guitarist wrote mainstays of the classic-rock canon like “Feelin’ Alright?” and “Only You Know and I Know.” He also had a successful solo career.
item.dc:creator: Jim Farber
item.media:description: Dave Mason performing in Chicago in 1980.

item.title: SpaceX Strikes Deal With Cursor for $60 Billion
item.description: The potential acquisition comes as Elon Musk’s rocket and satellite maker, which has been emphasizing artificial intelligence, is preparing to go public.
item.dc:creator: Erin Griffith, Mike Isaac and Ryan Mac
item.media:description: SpaceX said a deal with Cursor gave it the option to buy the artificial intelligence start-up, which makes code-writing software, for $60 billion later this year.

item.title: A.I. Is Eliminating Jobs on Wall Street
item.description: “A.I. gives us places to go we haven’t gone,” said one bank’s chief executive.
item.dc:creator: Rob Copeland
item.media:description: Brian T. Moynihan, Bank of America’s chief executive, said “eliminating work and applying technology” helped the bank shed 1,000 jobs last quarter.

item.title: A.I. ‘Hallucinations’ Created Errors in Court Filing, Top Law Firm Says
item.description: Sullivan & Cromwell apologized for submitting a court document that had fake citations created by artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Santul Nerkar
item.media:credit: John Taggart for The New York Times
item.media:description: Lawyers for Sullivan & Cromwell apologized for submitting a court filing that had fake citations created by artificial intelligence.

item.title: How Tim Cook’s Tech Uniform Helped Make Apple Trillions
item.description: The unassuming look of the outgoing Apple chief executive was an asset, as he navigated pop culture, the president and following Steve Jobs.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Gallagher
item.media:description: In his navy polos and dark jeans, Mr. Cook became a recognizable but unassuming figurehead of Apple. It may have been an asset for him to present as a kind of blank slate.

item.title: How to Save Academia
item.description: A Yale report offers some honest self-reflection on where the university went wrong.
item.dc:creator: Bret Stephens
item.media:credit: Christopher Capozziello for The New York Times

item.title: How Israel Lost Its Way and How Trump Can Save Lebanon
item.description: The smashing of a statue of Jesus by an Israeli soldier and the founding of four provocative settlements together show Israel’s thoughtless path forward.
item.dc:creator: Thomas L. Friedman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Michael Kosta Introduces Former Labor Secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer
item.description: “It’s always the ones you never heard of,” the late night host said on Tuesday’s “The Daily Show.”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: Late Show with Stephen Colbert (TV Program)
Late Night with Seth Meyers (TV Program)
The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)

item.title: Pope Leo Is Skipping Some of Africa’s Biggest Catholic Nations
item.description: As the pontiff arrives in the tiny authoritarian nation of Equatorial Guinea, some Catholics in Africa say they are excited about his visit but are feeling a little left out.
item.dc:creator: Ruth Maclean
item.media:description: Pope Leo XIV in Cameroon on Friday.

item.title: Man Is Charged With Providing Gun to Louisiana Shooter
item.description: The arrest came two days after a gunman carried out a rampage that left eight children dead in Shreveport and injured two adults.
item.dc:creator: Christina Morales
item.media:description: A vigil mourned eight children fatally shot in Shreveport, La.

item.title: With $116 Million Gift, National Gallery Will Send Its Art Around Nation
item.description: The large contribution from the billionaire collector Mitchell P. Rales is enabling long-term loans to smaller museums in perpetuity.
item.dc:creator: Robin Pogrebin

item.title: Serial Rapist Pleads Guilty to 2 Murders After Chewed Gum Links DNA to Crimes
item.description: Mitchell A. Gaff, 68, acknowledged killing two women who were sexually assaulted before being found dead in their Washington State apartments in the 1980s.
item.dc:creator: Livia Albeck-Ripka
item.media:description: A photo of Susan Vesey, who was killed in 1980.
